name: Ernst Toch
abstract: Ernst Toch (German: [ˈtɔχ]; 7 December 1887 – 1 October 1964) was an Austrian composer of European classical music and film scores, who from 1933 worked as an émigré in Paris, London and New York. He sought throughout his life to introduce new approaches to music.

works_created.t: Sonata for Cello and Piano, Op. 50
Sonatinetta for Flute, Clarinet, and Bassoon, Op. 84
The Princess and the Pea, Op. 43: Overture

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I played piano on a date for Morris, something that Ernst Toch had written... So I became a member of a very kind of funny, a lonesome, elite society.
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I read a book, Dr. Toch (PHONETIC). I used to study his book quite often

about_quotes.speaker: Abe Most
about_quotes.evidence: That’s Ernst Konitz or Ernst Toch — Toch was before Ernst Konitz.
about_quotes.doc_title: Abe Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999
